activities_projects.title: Stone and Thread Circular Motion Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand centripetal force and motion under gravitational influence
steps: Tie a small stone to one end of a thread
Hold the other end and whirl the stone in a circular path
Observe the circular motion
Release the thread and observe the direction of motion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand centripetal force and motion of objects under force

activities_projects.title: Free Fall Demonstration
activities_projects.objective: Understand the concept of free fall
steps: Hold a stone at a height
Release the stone without applying force
Observe its downward motion
Discuss the reason for the motion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that objects fall due to gravitational force
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct explanation of free fall (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Paper and Stone Falling Experiment
steps: Drop a paper sheet and a stone from the same height
Observe which reaches the ground first
Crumple the paper and repeat the experiment
Compare the observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the effect of air resistance on falling objects

activities_projects.title: Weight Calculation Activity
activities_projects.objective: Learn relation between mass and weight
steps: Write the formula W = m × g
Take different mass values such as 2 kg, 5 kg and 10 kg
Calculate weight using g = 9.8 m/s²
Record results in a table
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands relationship between mass and weight

activities_projects.title: Mass vs Weight Comparison Chart
steps: Draw a comparison table for mass and weight
Write definitions of both terms
Include SI units and measuring instruments
Provide examples
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands differences between mass and weight

activities_projects.title: Standing vs Lying on Sand Activity
steps: Stand on loose sand and observe the depth of footprints
Lie down on the sand
Observe the difference in sinking depth
Discuss why the results differ
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that pressure depends on area

activities_projects.title: Drawing Pin Pressure Activity
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate pressure due to small area
steps: Press a drawing pin into a board
Observe the sharp tip penetrating easily
Compare with pressing using the head
Discuss the effect of area on pressure

activities_projects.title: Floating Bottle Experiment
steps: Place a sealed empty bottle in a bucket of water
Observe that the bottle floats
Push the bottle deeper into water
Release it and observe its movement
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes buoyant force acting upward in fluids

activities_projects.title: Nail and Cork Floating Test
steps: Place an iron nail in water
Observe that it sinks
Place a cork on the water surface
Observe that it floats
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that density determines floating or sinking

activities_projects.title: Archimedes Principle Demonstration
activities_projects.objective: Understand buoyant force and displaced water
steps: Tie a stone to a spring balance
Note the reading in air
Slowly immerse the stone in water
Observe the change in reading
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands Archimedes’ principle and buoyant force
